title: 10 best herbal capsules for jaundice

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with jaundice because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The active compound curcumin in turmeric capsules has been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can help alleviate symptoms such as yellowing of the skin and eyes, fatigue, and dark urine associated with jaundice. Additionally, curcumin has been found to improve liver function by enhancing glutathione levels, thereby supporting the body's natural detoxification processes and promoting a healthy recovery from jaundice.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Turmeric's anti-inflammatory compound, curcumin, helps reduce inflammation in the liver and gallbladder, which can contribute to jaundice symptoms like yellowing of the skin and eyes.
Antioxidant property: Turmeric's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can damage liver cells and contribute to liver dysfunction, thereby reducing the severity of jandice-related liver damage.
Immunomodulatory property: Turmeric's immunomodulatory effects help modulate the immune response, which can aid in reducing inflammation and improving liver function in cases of jaundice.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of turmeric are rich in curcumin, a potent compound with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate symptoms of jaundice.
Roots: Turmeric roots contain volatile oils and phenolic compounds that aid in reducing liver damage and inflammation associated with jaundice.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality turmeric roots, drying them thoroughly for at least three days before use.
Grind the dried turmeric into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix one part of the ground turmeric with four parts of an edible oil such as coconut or olive oil in a small bowl.
Fill empty capsules with the turmeric oil mixture, using a capsule filling machine or manual method, aiming for 250mg per capsule.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months, keeping them away from direct sunlight.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle capsules helps with jaundice because they contain silymarin, a powerful antioxidant that protects liver cells from damage. Jaundice is often caused by liver inflammation or toxin-induced damage, and silymarin has been shown to reduce oxidative stress and promote liver regeneration. By taking herbal milk thistle capsules, individuals can support their liver's natural detoxification processes and alleviate symptoms of jaundice, such as yellowing of the skin and eyes, fever, and fatigue. This natural supplement may help restore liver function and promote overall health.
remedy_properties: Silymarin: A flavonoid complex that helps protect liver cells from damage, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress associated with jaundice.
Flavonoids: Antioxidant compounds that scavenge free radicals, preventing liver damage and promoting the body's natural detoxification processes to reduce bilirubin levels.
Saponins: Compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms such as itching and pain associated with jaundice.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Milk thistle seeds contain silymarin, a flavonoid complex that helps protect the liver from damage and promotes its regeneration.
Roots: The roots of milk thistle have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to support liver function and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried milk thistle flowers and leaves from certified organic sources.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm wellventilated area for 24 hours.
Grind 20 grams of dried milk thistle into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered milk thistle with 100 milliliters of 95 percent ethyl alcohol in a glass jar for 2 weeks.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth filter paper and fill empty gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of the extracted herbal mixture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsules helps with jaundice because they contain choline, a natural compound that has been shown to help remove excess bilirubin from the liver. Jaundice occurs when there is an overload of bilirubin in the blood, causing yellowing of the skin and eyes. Dandelion's choline-rich properties stimulate bile production and flow, allowing bilirubin to be excreted more efficiently, thereby reducing jaundice symptoms such as itching and discomfort. Additionally, dandelion's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties help soothe the liver and reduce inflammation associated with jaundice.
remedy_properties: Choleretic property: Dandelion capsules stimulate the production and secretion of bile, which helps to dissolve bilirubin and remove it from the body, reducing symptoms of jaundice.

Laxative property: The fiber content in dandelion capsules helps to promote regular bowel movements, preventing constipation and allowing for effective elimination of bilirubin-rich waste products that can contribute to jaundice.

Antioxidant property: Dandelion capsules contain antioxidants that help protect the liver from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate jaundice symptoms and slow down the body's natural detoxification processes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and bitter compounds that help stimulate liver function and reduce inflammation, making them effective in treating jaundice.
Fruits: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help alleviate symptoms such as fever, pain, and discomfort associated with jaundice.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion roots and leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated weighing about 100 grams.
Wash the harvested dandelion parts thoroughly with cold running water to remove dirt and debris rinsing for 10 minutes.
Dry the washed dandelion parts by spreading them out in a single layer on paper towels allowing air circulation and monitoring moisture levels daily until crispy.
Grind the dried dandelion parts into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle to achieve a smooth texture taking about 10 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with approximately 250-500 milligrams of the ground dandelion powder carefully pouring it into each capsule.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with jaundice because they possess potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The capsules contain a compound called centipedic acid, which has been shown to reduce bilirubin levels in the blood, a common indicator of jaundice. Additionally, the capsules have been found to improve liver function by increasing the activity of enzymes involved in bilirubin metabolism, thereby helping to alleviate symptoms of jaundice such as yellowing of the skin and eyes. This natural remedy has been traditionally used in China for centuries to treat various liver disorders, including jaundice.
remedy_properties: Silymarin: Silymarin, a flavonoid complex found in Maidenhair Tree capsules, has been shown to reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress, which can alleviate symptoms of jaundice by protecting the liver from damage.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Maidenhair Tree capsules have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce bilirubin levels and prevent its deposition in tissues, thereby relieving the yellowing of skin and eyes characteristic of jaundice.

Polyphenols: Polyphenols in Maidenhair Tree capsules have been found to enhance liver function by increasing the production of bile and reducing the activity of enzymes that break down bilirubin, thus promoting the elimination of toxic substances from the body.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the maidenhair tree contain bioactive compounds that have been traditionally used to treat liver and gallbladder disorders, including jaundice.
Barks: The bark of the maidenhair tree is rich in tannins, which help to reduce inflammation and promote the body's natural healing processes, making it effective for treating jaundice.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality ginkgo biloba leaves from mature trees in late fall or early spring.
Dry the ginkgo biloba leaves using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 24 hours or air-drying for 7-10 days.
Grind 30 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice grinder.
Mix the ground ginkgo biloba with 100ml of distilled water to create a paste and let it steep for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and fill sterile capsules with the resulting liquid extract at a dosage of 240-320mg per capsule.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Barberry capsules helps with jaundice because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The herb contains berberine, a bioactive compound that has been shown to reduce bilirubin levels in the blood by inhibiting the activity of enzymes involved in bilirubin production. Additionally, barberry's immunomodulatory effects help to enhance the body's natural defenses against infection, which can contribute to jaundice. By reducing inflammation and promoting immune function, barberry capsules may alleviate symptoms and support recovery from jaundice.
remedy_properties: Berberine: Berberine, a key compound found in barberry, has potent antiviral and antibacterial properties, which can help combat infections that may contribute to jaundice.
Anthraquinones: Barberry's anthraquinone compounds have been shown to stimulate the gallbladder and bile ducts, potentially helping to reduce bilirubin buildup in the blood, a hallmark of jaundice.
Flavonoids: The flavonoid content in barberry may help protect the liver from oxidative damage, inflammation, and fibrosis, which can contribute to liver dysfunction and jaundice.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their high concentration of berberine, a compound with potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that helps alleviate symptoms of jaundice.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes, the underground stems of the plant, contain a higher concentration of berberine than other parts, making them effective in treating jaundice-related liver and gallbladder issues.
remedy_recipe: Collect high-quality dried berberis vulgaris roots in the required quantity for preparation.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a grinder or mortar and pestle.
Measure out 250-500 milligrams of the ground root powder and fill empty capsules with the measured amount.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature away from direct sunlight for up to six months.
Take two capsules twice daily with warm water after meals for four to six weeks or as recommended by a healthcare professional.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curly dock capsules helps with jaundice because they contain anthraquinone glycosides, which have been traditionally used to treat liver and digestive issues. These compounds have been shown to stimulate bile flow and improve liver function, reducing the levels of bilirubin in the blood that cause jaundice. Additionally, curly dock's anti-inflammatory properties may help soothe the liver and reduce oxidative stress, further supporting its ability to alleviate symptoms of jaundice.
remedy_properties: Anthraquinones: These compounds have been shown to increase bile flow and reduce bilirubin levels in the blood, helping to alleviate the symptoms of jaundice.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Curly Dock capsules have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the liver from oxidative stress and damage, and may also improve liver function and reduce inflammation.

Tannins: Tannins in Curly Dock capsules have been shown to possess antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help combat viral hepatitis infections that can cause jaundice and other liver-related issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anthraquinone glycosides, which help to reduce bilirubin levels and alleviate symptoms of jaundice.
Rhyzomes: Contain a high concentration of saponins, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can benefit patients with jaundice.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried curly dock leaves from reputable sources in sufficient quantities for the desired number of capsules.
Chop dried curly dock leaves into small pieces to increase surface area and facilitate extraction.
Mix 20-30 grams of chopped curly dock leaves with 400ml of boiling water and let it steep for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container discarding solids and reserving liquid.
Fill gelatin capsules with 0.25-0.50 grams of dried curly dock herb and store them in airtight containers at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock capsules helps with jaundice because of its unique properties that support liver health. The plant's roots contain inulin, a prebiotic fiber that feeds beneficial gut bacteria, promoting a healthy gut-liver axis. Additionally, burdock's saponins have been shown to stimulate the production of bile and improve liver function, helping to reduce bilirubin levels and alleviate jaundice symptoms such as yellowing of the skin and eyes. By supporting liver detoxification and improving bile flow, burdock capsules can help to effectively address jaundice and promote overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Choleretic and cholekinetic properties: Burdock root stimulates the production of bile in the liver and improves its flow through the bile ducts, which helps to reduce bilirubin levels in the blood and alleviate jaundice symptoms.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: The antioxidants present in burdock root help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress and inflammation, which can worsen jaundice symptoms such as itching and fatigue.

Laxative properties: Burdock root has a gentle laxative effect, which helps to relieve constipation, a common symptom of jaundice caused by impaired bile flow or liver dysfunction, by stimulating bowel movements and reducing straining during defecation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, burdock roots have been traditionally used to treat liver disorders and jaundice.
Seeds: The seeds contain lignans, which have been shown to have detoxifying properties and help reduce bilirubin levels in the blood.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried arctium lappa root material from reputable sources weighing 250g for each capsule batch.
Clean and dry the harvested root material using a food dehydrator set at 60°c for 4 hours.
Grind the dried root material into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle within 10 minutes.
Mix the powdered root material with gelatin capsules in a 1:1 ratio by weight adding 250mg of gelatin to each capsule.
Fill and seal the filled capsules immediately storing them in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort capsules helps with jaundice because it has been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of liver dysfunction. The herb contains hyperforin, a compound that has been shown to increase the breakdown of bilirubin in the liver, reducing levels of this pigment in the blood and alleviating yellowing of the skin and eyes characteristic of jaundice. By supporting healthy liver function, St John's wort may help to clear bilirubin from the bloodstream, relieving symptoms and promoting overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Antidepressant and anxiolytic compounds: St. John's Wort contains active ingredients such as hyperforin and hypericin, which have been shown to exhibit antidepressant and anxiolytic effects by increasing neurotransmitter levels in the brain.

Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties: The plant has been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress caused by various conditions.

Antiviral activity: St. John's Wort has been shown to exhibit antiviral activity against certain viruses, including herpes simplex virus type 2 (HSV-2) and human immunodeficiency virus (HIV), although the efficacy of this property in clinical settings is still debated.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots have been traditionally used to treat liver and gallbladder issues, making them suitable for treating jaundice.
Barks: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, barks have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of jaundice such as itching and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh st. john's wort leaves and flowers, ensuring at least 2 ounces of dried material for the batch.
Clean and dry the harvested material using a gentle air current or paper towels, removing any debris or impurities.
Combine the cleaned and dried st. john's wort with one tablespoon of vegetable glycerin in a clean glass jar, allowing it to infuse for 2-4 weeks shaking daily.
Strain the infused mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a separate container, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid extract.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the st. john's wort extract, using a digital scale to accurately measure the dosage for each capsule.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with jaundice because they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The active ingredients in licorice root, such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the liver, which can contribute to the development of jaundice. Additionally, licorice has been shown to improve liver function and promote bile flow, which can help to alleviate symptoms of jaundice such as yellowing of the skin and eyes.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin, a compound found in licorice root, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce liver damage and inflammation associated with jaundice.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in licorice extract have been shown to have antiviral and antibacterial activities, which can help combat viral or bacterial infections that may contribute to jaundice.
Liquiritin: Liquiritin, another compound found in licorice root, has been reported to have liver-protective effects, helping to prevent liver damage and promote recovery from jaundice.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that has been shown to reduce bilirubin levels and alleviate symptoms associated with jaundice.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes contain triterpenoids, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory properties and can help reduce liver damage caused by jaundice.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh glycyrrhiza glabra roots and rinse them thoroughly under running water.
Cut the roots into small pieces and dry them in a warm, well-ventilated area for 7 days.
Grind the dried root pieces into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 500mg of powdered licorice root with 100mg of gelatin and add 10ml of distilled water to create a filling mixture.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the filling mixture and seal them immediately to preserve freshness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milkweed capsules helps with jaundice because they contain saponins, which have been shown to stimulate bile production and flow in the liver. This can help reduce bilirubin levels in the blood, a common indicator of jaundice. Additionally, milkweed's bitter compounds may also help increase gallbladder contractions, promoting the release of stored bile into the small intestine, where it can aid in fat digestion and absorption. By supporting liver function and increasing bile flow, herbal milkweed capsules can provide natural relief from jaundice symptoms.
remedy_properties: Choleretic properties: Milkweed contains compounds that can stimulate the production of bile in the liver, which may help to improve liver function and reduce jaundice symptoms by increasing the flow of bile into the small intestine (why: increased bile production helps eliminate bilirubin from the body).

Antioxidant activity: Milkweed contains antioxidants like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can help protect the liver cells from damage caused by free radicals and oxidative stress, potentially alleviating jaundice symptoms (why: antioxidant defenses may reduce liver inflammation and cell damage).

Anti-inflammatory effects: Milkweed's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce liver inflammation and swelling associated with jaundice, improving overall liver function and reducing symptoms like fatigue, nausea, and dark urine (why: reduced inflammation can improve liver function and alleviate symptoms).
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, which help reduce liver inflammation and improve bilirubin metabolism.
Rhizomes: Contain bioactive compounds that inhibit the formation of bilirubin-induced liver damage and promote liver regeneration.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh asclepias syriaca leaves and stems on a sunny day in late summer for best quality.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator on low heat for 8 hours or until crisp.
Grind 100g of dried milkweed into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 part ground milkweed with 2 parts gelatin capsule filler in a bowl, then fill capsules with the mixture.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chicory capsules helps with jaundice because of its unique properties that promote liver detoxification. The herb chicory, also known as Cichorium intybus, has been traditionally used to treat various liver and gallbladder disorders, including jaundice. Chicory capsules contain bioactive compounds that stimulate bile production, improve liver function, and enhance the body's natural detoxification processes. This can help reduce bilirubin levels in the blood, alleviating symptoms of jaundice such as yellowing of the skin and eyes. Regular consumption of chicory capsules may provide relief from jaundice by supporting healthy liver function and promoting overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Cholagogic property: Chicory capsules stimulate the gallbladder to release bile, which helps to remove bilirubin from the bloodstream and reduce jaundice symptoms.
Laxative property: Chicory's ability to increase bowel movements can help eliminate excess bilirubin and other waste products in the body, further alleviating jaundice symptoms.
Antioxidant property: Chicory's antioxidants help protect liver cells from damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to the development of jaundice.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chicory roots contain inulin, a prebiotic fiber that helps regulate liver function and reduce bilirubin levels, making them effective in treating jaundice.
Rhyzomes: Chicory rhyzomes have been traditionally used to treat liver and gallbladder disorders, including jaundice, due to their ability to stimulate bile production and improve digestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of dried cichorium intybus leaves from a reputable supplier or grow your own.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the powdered herb with 1000mg of gelatin capsules in a small bowl, filling each capsule by hand for 15 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months, ensuring optimal potency.
Take two capsules twice daily with water as needed, adjusting dosage based on individual response and physician guidance.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Siberian ginseng capsules helps with jaundice because of its remarkable ability to stimulate the liver and improve its function. The herb's adaptogenic properties allow it to enhance the body's natural defense mechanisms, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with jaundice such as yellowing of the skin and eyes. Additionally, Siberian ginseng has been shown to increase blood circulation and reduce inflammation, further contributing to its potential in treating this condition. remedy_properties: Adaptogenic Property: Siberian Ginseng capsules have been shown to adapt the body's physiological processes to stress, including liver damage caused by jaundice, thereby helping to reduce inflammation and promote recovery.

Antioxidant Activity: The antioxidant compounds present in Siberian Ginseng capsules may help protect the liver from further damage by neutralizing free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress and tissue damage associated with jaundice.

Immunomodulatory Effect: Siberian Ginseng capsules have been found to modulate the immune response, which can help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the liver, thereby alleviating symptoms of jaundice.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and other bioactive compounds, which help to reduce liver damage and inflammation associated with jaundice.
Rhyzomes: Containing high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents, which can help to soothe the liver and promote its function.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality eleutherococcus senticosus roots and dry them thoroughly for six hours in a warm oven.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder for 10 minutes.
Mix one part of the powdered root with two parts of potato starch to create a uniform blend and store it in an airtight container for 24 hours.
Fill clean, size zero hard gelatin capsules with approximately 400mg of the blended mixture and seal them immediately for 15 minutes.
Store the finished capsules in an airtight container in a cool, dry place for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ashwagandha capsules helps with jaundice because they possess potent antioxidant properties that help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the liver. Jaundice occurs when there is an accumulation of bilirubin in the blood, which can cause yellowing of the skin and eyes. Ashwagandha's ability to scavenge free radicals and protect the liver from damage helps to restore normal liver function, thereby reducing bilirubin levels and alleviating jaundice symptoms. remedy_properties: Adaptogenic property: Ashwagandha helps to reduce stress and anxiety associated with jaundice, promoting overall well-being and alleviating symptoms such as fatigue.
Antioxidant activity: Ashwagandha's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can damage liver cells during jaundice, thereby reducing oxidative stress and inflammation.

Hepatoprotective property: Ashwagandha has been shown to have a protective effect on the liver by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory compounds and promoting the repair of damaged liver tissue.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Ashwagandha are used to make medicinal capsules as they contain a high concentration of withanolides, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce liver damage in jaundice patients.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Ashwagandha are used due to their rich content of bioactive compounds, including glycosides, terpenoids, and saponins, which may help alleviate symptoms of jaundice by promoting liver function and detoxification.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality withania somnifera roots and dry them in shade for four weeks.
Grind the dried roots into fine powder using a grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix one part of the powdered root with two parts of lactose powder in a bowl.
Fill 10 empty gelatin capsules with approximately 0.5 grams of the root-lactose mixture each, sealing them immediately.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Guggul capsules helps with jaundice because they contain Commiphora mukul, a natural herb that has been traditionally used to treat various liver-related disorders. The herb's active compounds, such as gum guggulu and commiphyrin, have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce bilirubin levels in the blood, thus alleviating jaundice symptoms like yellowing of the skin and eyes. Additionally, guggul capsules may also improve liver function and promote the body's natural detoxification processes, further supporting its effectiveness in managing jaundice.
remedy_properties: Inflammation reduction: Guggul's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce inflammation in the liver and gallbladder, which can alleviate symptoms of jaundice such as yellowing of the skin and eyes.

Bile duct stimulation: Guggul's ability to stimulate bile ducts increases bile production and flow, which can help to remove bilirubin from the blood and reduce its accumulation in tissues, thereby relieving jaundice symptoms.
Antioxidant activity: Guggul's antioxidant property helps to neutralize free radicals that can damage liver cells and exacerbate jaundice symptoms, promoting overall liver health and function.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in guggulsterones, which help reduce liver inflammation and promote bile secretion, aiding in the treatment of jaundice.
Roots: Containing bioactive compounds like glycosides and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate jaundice symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried commiphora mukul resin from a reputable supplier.
Grind the dried resin into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix one part of the powdered resin with two parts of rice flour in a bowl to create a uniform blend.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the blended mixture and seal immediately.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Neem capsules helps with jaundice because of its potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The capsules contain neem's active compound, azadirachtin, which effectively breaks down bilirubin, a yellow pigment that builds up in the blood during jaundice, causing yellowing of the skin and eyes. Additionally, neem has been shown to enhance liver function, helping to remove excess bilirubin from the body and reduce its concentration in the blood. This natural approach helps alleviate jaundice symptoms, promoting overall health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial Properties: Neem's antimicrobial compounds, such as azadirachtin, destroy harmful bacteria, viruses, and fungi that cause infections and contribute to jaundice.

Anti-inflammatory Properties: Neem's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation in the liver and bile ducts, which helps alleviate symptoms like yellowing of the skin and eyes, and abdominal pain.

Choleretic Properties: Neem's choleretic properties stimulate the production and secretion of bile, which helps to remove bilirubin from the blood, reducing its levels and alleviating jaundice symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the neem plant are rich in alkaloids and saponins that help to reduce bilirubin levels in the blood, making them effective in treating jaundice.
Seeds: Neem seeds contain azadirachtin, a compound that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce liver inflammation and alleviate symptoms of jaundice.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried leaves of azadirachta indica, ensuring quality and potency for subsequent use.
Clean and dry the harvested material by gently brushing away debris and allowing air circulation for several hours.
Grind the cleaned material into a fine powder using a grinder or mortar and pestle, taking approximately 30 minutes to achieve desired consistency.
Mix the powdered azadirachta indica with suitable capsules or fillers in a 1:2 ratio by weight, filling each capsule carefully and accurately weighing for precision.
Store the filled capsules in airtight containers at room temperature or refrigerate if exposed to direct sunlight or high temperatures, ensuring freshness and potency for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thorow-wax capsules helps with jaundice because they are made from the leaves of the Euphorbia thoryoides plant, which has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various liver and digestive disorders. The capsule's active compounds, such as glycosides and flavonoids, have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce bilirubin levels and improve liver function, thus alleviating the symptoms of jaundice. Additionally, the capsules' ability to stimulate digestion and eliminate toxins may also contribute to their beneficial effects on jaundice patients.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Thorow-wax capsules contain antioxidants that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver, which is often associated with jaundice.

Choleretic and cholagogue properties: The herb's ability to stimulate bile production and flow can help relieve symptoms of jaundice such as yellowing of the skin and eyes (icterus) by promoting the elimination of bilirubin from the body.

Anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects: Thorow-wax capsules may also contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce liver inflammation and modulate the immune response, which is often compromised in cases of jaundice.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, which help to detoxify the liver and reduce bilirubin levels in patients with jaundice.
Buds: Containing bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of jaundice such as itching and skin rashes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality bupleurum chinense roots and cut them into small pieces to ensure uniform drying.
Dry the cut bupleurum chinense roots in a shaded area or using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 24 hours.
Grind the dried bupleurum chinense roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 500g of the powdered bupleurum chinense with 1000g of excipients such as potato starch or cornstarch in a large bowl.
Fill #00 empty capsules with the mixture and seal them tightly to ensure freshness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover capsules helps with jaundice because they contain isoflavones, particularly biochanin A, which have been shown to improve liver function and reduce bilirubin levels in the blood. Jaundice occurs when there is an accumulation of bilirubin, a yellow pigment produced during the breakdown of old red blood cells. The isoflavones in red clover capsules help to enhance the body's natural detoxification processes, thereby reducing bilirubin levels and alleviating symptoms of jaundice such as yellowing of the skin and eyes.
remedy_properties: Isoflavones: Red clover contains isoflavones, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the liver, helping to alleviate symptoms of jaundice such as itching and yellowing of the skin.
Anthocyanins: The anthocyanin content in red clover has antioxidant properties that help protect the liver from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to jaundice.

Saponins: Red clover's saponins have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may help reduce inflammation and infection in the liver and bile ducts, both of which are associated with jaundice.
remedy_parts: Roots: Red clover roots have been traditionally used to treat liver and gallbladder issues, making them a suitable part for medicinal capsules to alleviate jaundice symptoms.

Flowers: The flowers of the red clover plant contain saponins, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce bilirubin levels in the blood and alleviate jaundice symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality trifolium pratense flowers at peak bloom and air-dry them within three days for optimal potency preservation.
Grind 10 grams of dried trifolium pratense flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered trifolium pratense with 50 milliliters of distilled water to create a concentrated herbal infusion and steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth or coffee filter and transfer the liquid extract into gelatin capsules filled with 500 milligrams each.
Store the finished capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months while monitoring expiration date.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with jaundice because they contain a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has been shown to reduce bilirubin levels in the blood. Bilirubin is a yellow pigment that builds up in the liver and bloodstream when there's an imbalance of red blood cells, leading to jaundice. The antiviral properties of licorice capsules also help combat infections that may exacerbate jaundice, while its anti-inflammatory effects reduce liver inflammation, allowing for better bile flow and elimination of excess bilirubin from the body.
remedy_properties: Licoricidin: Has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce liver damage and inflammation caused by jaundice, promoting a faster recovery.
Glycyrrhizin: Inhibits the activity of certain enzymes involved in bilirubin production, reducing the buildup of bilirubin in the blood that causes jaundice.
Isoliquiritigenin: Exhibits anti-bilirubinate properties, which help decrease bilirubin levels in the blood and alleviate symptoms of jaundice.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps reduce bilirubin levels and alleviate symptoms of jaundice.
Barks: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe liver inflammation and promote recovery from jaundice.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh glycyrrhiza uralensis roots in the morning after dew has dried for optimal quality and quantity.
Cleanse and cut the harvested roots into small pieces to remove any dirt or debris.
Soak the root pieces in 20% ethanol solution at a ratio of 1:5 for two hours to enhance bioavailability.
Dry the soaked root pieces in a rotary dryer set at 40°c for four hours or until completely dry.
Fill clean gelatin capsules with 200mg of dried glycyrrhiza uralensis powder and store in an airtight container for up to six months.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for jaundice are a natural remedy that combines various plant-based ingredients to alleviate symptoms associated with this liver disorder. These capsules help by promoting liver function, reducing bilirubin levels, and improving digestive health. Examples of herbal capsules that aid in jaundice treatment include Milk Thistle and Turmeric, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. By taking these capsules, patients experience improved appetite, reduced fatigue, and a clearer complexion, allowing them to resume their daily activities with greater ease and confidence.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat jaundice with herbal capsules is to combine Turmeric capsules with Milk Thistle extract. Turmeric has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce liver inflammation, while Milk Thistle extract stimulates the production of liver enzymes and helps regenerate damaged liver cells. Take 2-3 capsules twice a day for 1-2 weeks or as directed by a healthcare professional. Consult a doctor before using herbal remedies to ensure safe treatment.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to jaundice that are treated with herbal capsules are various liver and gallbladder disorders, such as hepatitis, fatty liver, and cholestasis. Herbal capsules containing ingredients like milk thistle, turmeric, and ginger are often used to support liver health, reduce inflammation, and improve bile flow. These herbs may also help alleviate symptoms like jaundice-like skin discoloration, nausea, and fatigue associated with these conditions.
